## Summary

Stacks on #721 and adds the first local-first/offline layer for the Revise Workbench.

The goal is that once an author has opened a manuscript’s Revise Workbench on a device, that device can still show the queue, evidence, A/B/C proposals, historical ledger entries, and new decisions if the internet connection drops.

## What changed

- Caches the loaded workbench payload in `localStorage` per manuscript/evaluation.
- Caches Revision Ledger entries locally per manuscript/evaluation.
- Restores cached queue + ledger on page load when available.
- Shows online/offline status in the workbench header.
- Shows local cache status and last cached timestamp.
- Marks new local decisions as `Pending sync`.
- Preserves local author decisions immediately on click, before any future server sync.
- Keeps undo local-first as well.

## Product doctrine

Author decisions must not disappear because a network connection drops.

The workbench should behave like a serious writing tool:

- instant local decision capture;
- offline review after the device has synced once;
- pending-sync status when online persistence is not yet complete;
- server persistence later becomes the cross-device source of truth.

## Device behavior

Offline access is per-device.

- If the author opens the queue on a laptop while online, the laptop can show it offline later.
- If the author opens the queue on a phone while online, the phone can show it offline later.
- A device that never loaded/synced the manuscript before going offline cannot have that queue locally.

## Scope

Local/offline cache only.

No server decision table/API yet.
No dashboard analytics yet.
No cross-device sync yet.
No pricing, credits, checkout, scoring, evaluation pipeline, report rendering, Agent Readiness, or Storygate runtime changes.

## Dependency

This PR is stacked on #721 and should be reviewed/merged after #720 and #721.

## Next PR after this

Add the server-side Revision Ledger decision API/table so pending local decisions sync across devices and feed dashboard analytics.

## Acceptance criteria

- Workbench queue is cached locally after successful load.
- Revision Ledger entries are cached locally.
- Refreshing the page restores local ledger history.
- Offline status is visible.
- New decisions made while offline remain visible.
- New decisions are marked Pending sync until server persistence lands.
- Undo updates the local cache.
